hypothesis.mechanism: The cluster's blockers are downstream derived-state conditions in curl's URL/HTTP parsers (byte/header-line counters, linked-list head pointers, socket revents flags) that only become nonzero/non-null after the input clears a CHAIN of sequential opaque multi-byte literal CMPs (scheme 'pop3+-', host '127.0.0.1', 'HTTP/' framing, header tokens). I2S (cmplog and value_profile_cmplog, both carrying I2SRandReplace) logs each literal operand from the CmpLogObserver and substitutes it into the input one gate at a time, so it clears the chain in roughly a number of mutations linear in the chain length. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace: naive has only blind havoc, and value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards Hamming progress per single CMP but cannot climb an exact multi-byte equality nor stack independent sequential equalities, so both must land every literal byte by chance. The has_axis advantage is therefore identical across cmplog and value_profile_cmplog (same substitution mutator) and grows as more sequential literal gates are chained before the downstream trap.
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN (compile-time -D) — the number of sequential opaque 4-byte literal gates the input must clear, in order, before the downstream derived-state trap fires.
hypothesis.predicted_direction: has_axis fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) outperform lacks_axis fuzzers (naive, value_profile); the crash-count gap grows with LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN

predicted_endpoints.LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=1: Single 4-byte literal gate. has_axis resolves quickly via one I2S substitution; lacks_axis may land a small number of crashes by blind chance (~1/256^4 per exec), so the gap is modest.
predicted_endpoints.LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN=8: Eight sequential 4-byte literal gates (~1/256^32 for blind search). lacks_axis expected at zero crashes with certainty; has_axis still reaches the trap via per-gate substitution, so the gap is maximal.

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: flat-high then gently declining (resolves at every chain length; time-to-trap rises slowly with LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN as more substitutions are needed)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile_cmplog: flat-high, tracking cmplog (same I2SRandReplace substitution); may equal or slightly exceed cmplog via the added VP feedback
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.naive: monotone-decreasing to zero (crash count collapses as ~1/256^(4*LITERAL_CHAIN_LEN); zero by chain length 2)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: monotone-decreasing to zero (no operand substitution; CMP_MAP cannot climb stacked exact equalities)

hypothesis.tradeoff_observation: None expected: this is a pure I2S_pro cluster, all seven members show only the I2S technique with cmplog>naive and value_profile_cmplog>value_profile on the same axis. value_profile_cmplog may post a higher absolute crash count than cmplog at a given chain length because it also carries VP feedback, but that is a within-has_axis offset difference and does not invert the stratification.

verification.dockerfile: one per scan value, see deployed_targets
notes: Knob is chain length (number of sequential opaque 4-byte literal gates), not constant width, per the cluster definition: the real blocker is a downstream derived-state condition reached only after several sequential literal CMPs are satisfied, so the I2S advantage scales with how many gates must be cleared in sequence.
Each gate is a single exact 4-byte memcmp equality with no partial-match coverage edge; the trap fires only on full-chain success, so naive/value_profile are given no gradient to climb.
